internal_control_name: End-user Access to Applications and Data

mappings.reason: Configuring data access control lists directly implements the permissions required to view or modify data, satisfying IAM-38's authorization objective.
Enforcing device lockout after failed attempts secures the password authentication mechanism required by IAM-38 against brute force attacks.
Requiring unique passwords for all enterprise assets directly satisfies IAM-38's mandate for unique identifiers and passwords prior to access.
Disabling dormant accounts ensures the unique identifiers remain valid and authorized, supporting the integrity of IAM-38's authentication requirement.
Centralizing account management through a directory service enables the scalable maintenance of unique identifiers required by IAM-38.
Establishing an access granting process governs the enabling of access to applications, which IAM-38 requires prior to use.
Establishing an access revoking process governs the lifecycle of access, ensuring permissions are removed when no longer authorized under IAM-38.
Requiring MFA for externally-exposed applications enhances the password authentication mechanism for a subset of IAM-38's application scope.
Centralizing access control through a directory or SSO provider directly implements the authentication mechanism required by IAM-38 prior to enabling access.
Defining and maintaining role-based access control directly implements the permissions structure required by IAM-38 to view or modify data.
